title: 2025 Cirrus SR20 G7+ Premium
description:
Meet N580CA, a December 2025 Cirrus G7+ SR20 that redefines what “new airplane smell” really means. This aircraft is exactly that—brand new, untouched, and ready to deliver the full next-generation Cirrus experience.
The G7+ platform takes everything Cirrus does best and turns the dial up another notch, with a refined cockpit, breathtaking displays, and the latest evolution of Cirrus safety innovation. Front and center is the state-of-the-art Safe Return system, quietly standing by as the ultimate safety net, capable of autonomously landing the aircraft at the push of a button. It’s not just peace of mind—it’s a game-changer for owners, families, and passengers alike.
N580CA is stunning to look at and even better to sit in, with the kind of fit, finish, and modern design that makes every flight feel like a special occasion. This is a zero-compromise airplane: efficient, responsive, confidence-inspiring, and unmistakably Cirrus from the moment you power up.
Why wait months—or longer—for a factory slot when this beautiful 2025 G7+ SR20 is ready today? N580CA is the rare opportunity to step straight into the future of personal aviation, skip the line, and start flying one of the most advanced SR20s ever built right now.

The Cessna 210 Centurion is a six-seat, single-engine, piston aircraft that was first flown in January 1957 and produced by Cessna. It entered the market in 1960.
This model was designed similarly to the Cessna 182 but includes retractable landing gear, a swept tail, a new wing, and a hydraulic gear system. The fuselage was extended and made wider, while the flaps were extended to allow for a decreased landing speed.
The Cessna 210 was produced until 1986, and a total of 575 were built.
